***
[2:53] In Form I the system opens and authentic newness pours in from
outside so that the psyche encounters—not itself as world—but the divine
other rich with a mysterious infinitude of possibilities—and the dialogue
between the psyche and this authentic other begins and from there grows into
a different sort of information exchange, which is not just a signal from the
psyche boosted and enhanced and returned. The given psyche is now no
longer essentially alone.
 
[2:54] The transformation from the inauthentic to the authentic mode requires
the sacrificial death of the illusory psyche, a difficult price to pay—difficult to
make because for a little time it means the extinction of the person. He must
work_title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
